# Reliabilism and the reliability of cognitive processes

**Node Type:** synthesis
**Node ID:** e6e1c77d-9a12-4a97-8f7f-91adb311a67b
**Depth:** 5
**Terminal:** No

**Parent:** [Is the requirement of justified true belief sufficient to account for knowledge in all contexts?](is-the-requirement-of-justified-true-belief-sufficient-to-account-for-knowledge-in-all-contexts-antithesis-30e1a1bb-b627-4f5b-85e0-76a4355dbb91.md)

## Content

**Justification of a belief depends on the reliability of the process that produced it**, **A belief is known if it is true and generated by a reliable process**, **Reliable cognitive processes typically produce true beliefs**, **Justification does not depend on foundational beliefs but on process reliability**
